title: Clone and remote configuration deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Clone and remote configuration; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Clone and remote configuration: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Clone creates a live shared folder where local edits immediately alter the remote repository.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. A clone is an independent local repository initialized from remote data; later fetch and push operations synchronize selected references explicitly.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. A clone is an independent local repository initialized from remote data; later fetch and push operations synchronize selected references explicitly.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Clone and remote configuration instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: A clone is an independent local repository initialized from remote data; later fetch and push operations synchronize selected references explicitly.
Clone creates a live shared folder where local edits immediately alter the remote repository.

answer.explanation: A clone is an independent local repository initialized from remote data; later fetch and push operations synchronize selected references explicitly.
